agent_outputs.raw: Three issues make the hospital-center backtest insufficient for mobile deployment: (1) verification bias from referral-based labeling, (2) device and operator shift to mobile setting, (3) threshold-level referral capacity analysis absent for mobile lower-prevalence population. A prospective mobile pilot with independent adjudication is needed. QC rule is a sensible safeguard, not a hidden flaw. Lower prevalence requires recalibration, not disqualification. VERDICT: critique_wins

Verification bias, device shift, and missing threshold analysis are three distinct gaps that prevent the current evidence from justifying deployment. The retrospective evidence supports a prospective pilot, not direct deployment. VERDICT: critique_wins

The three critical gaps — label verification bias, device-operator distribution shift, referral capacity tradeoff at threshold level — are each genuine and are each required before deployment can be justified. Prospective mobile validation with unbiased adjudication is the necessary next step. VERDICT: critique_wins

synthesis: All three assessors unanimously find critique wins. The three core issues are consistently identified: verification-biased labels, device-operator shift, and missing threshold-level referral capacity analysis. All assessors correctly treat QC routing and lower prevalence as valid considerations rather than automatic disqualifiers. ETD constraint: critique wins.
all_issues_raised: verification bias — labels from referred patients only
device and operator distribution shift to mobile setting
referral threshold analysis missing for mobile lower-prevalence population

empirical_test.condition: Prospective mobile-van study with masked specialist adjudication on all sampled patients, measuring sensitivity and referral volume at planned threshold in mobile lower-prevalence population
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Sensitivity below prespecified floor at planned threshold; device shift degrades readable-image scores; or escalation rate substantially reduces capacity benefit
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Sensitivity maintained with at least 20% referral reduction and manageable escalation
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Performance adequate on readable images but escalation rate prevents net benefit assessment
